quoteFirst: Holmes led the way into the feed store, and we met the proprietor, who strongly reminded me of Inspector Letstrayed and Egbert Bunbury by his general air of sleepy incompetence. It was now
quoteLast: , and after Holmes had warned old man Wuxley of his identity beneath the valet's livery, we decided to hide behind one of the barrels of bran that stood on one side of the store, and there await the coming of Demetrius with his booty.
title:
The Adventures of the Eleven Cuff-Buttons
Being one of the exciting episodes in the career of the famous detective Hemlock Holmes, as recorded by his friend Dr. Watson
author: Thierry, James Francis

quoteLast: ; I am _never_ late, Lady Dove, and, as you know, I never allow anyone to do my work.  Excuse me——"
title: Harum Scarum's Fortune
author: Stuart, Esmè, 1851-1934

quoteFirst: He was half an hour early at the Moana, wearing his best blue aloha shirt, his mustache trimmed, his fingers drumming on the bar. Gilbert brought him a Glenlivet and left him alone. At
quoteLast: , Daisy walked out of the hotel and down the wide steps. He knew her first by her walk, tall and careful, and then, as she approached, by her face which was fuller, more deeply lined, but still good humored and direct. They embraced beneath the banyan.
title: Joe Burke's Last Stand
author: Wetterau, John Moncure

title: Warren Commission (01 of 26): Hearings Vol. I (of 15)
author: United States. Warren Commission

quoteFirst: Commenced ascending another mass of similar rocky ranges; stopped at 3.40 two and a quarter miles further to look out a track to endeavour to get out of this awful place. Started again at
quoteLast: after spelling one and a quarter hours, could not get the animals over. Went back till 5.22 one mile on our track, or to sixteen and a half miles on bearing 100 degrees, to try another place, southerly and westerly along and over very rocky ranges till 6.
title: McKinlay's Journal of Exploration in the Interior of Australia
author: McKinlay, John, 1819-1872

quoteFirst: I begged Him, if it pleased Him, to give me sleep, and wake me at five. I laid down, slept, and when I awoke, looked at my watch, which was just
quoteLast: . I felt, and still feel my obligations to the Lord, and am resolved to commit myself to His guidance.--My birthday. I awoke a little after three, and arose at half-past four, with these words upon my mind, 'Who will consecrate his services this day unto the Lord?' My heart responds, 'I will.
title: Religion in Earnest: A Memorial of Mrs. Mary Lyth, of York
author: Lyth, J. (John), 1821-1886

quoteLast: he started to fidget with his rifle, and then suddenly springing up on the fire step with a muttered, "I'll send over a couple of souvenirs to Fritz, so that he'll miss me when I leave," he stuck his rifle over the top and fired two shots, when "crack" went a bullet and he tumbled off the step, fell into the mud at the bottom of the trench, and lay still in a huddled heap with a bullet hole in his forehead.
title:
"Over the Top," by an American Soldier Who Went
Together with Tommy's Dictionary of the Trenches
author: Empey, Arthur Guy, 1883-1963
